rules.explanation_template: This clause contains a non-compete restriction that prevents you from working in your field{duration_text}. In India, Section 27 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 declares such agreements VOID as they restrain lawful profession, trade, or business. The only exception is when selling a business with goodwill.
rules.recommendation: Remove this clause entirely. Non-compete agreements are unenforceable in India. You have a fundamental right to earn a livelihood in your chosen profession.
rules.legal_reference: Section 27: Every agreement by which anyone is restrained from exercising a lawful profession, trade or business of any kind, is to that extent void.
examples: Employee shall not engage in any competing business for 2 years after termination.
You agree not to work with any competitor during and after employment.
Employee shall refrain from joining competitors for 18 months.

rules.explanation_template: This clause restricts you from contacting clients or employees{duration_text}. While some courts have upheld limited non-solicitation clauses, overly broad restrictions may violate Section 27. Indian courts scrutinize post-employment restrictions heavily.
rules.recommendation: Negotiate to limit this clause to: (1) only during employment, or (2) a very short period (30-90 days) post-employment with specific client lists, not blanket restrictions.
rules.legal_reference: Section 27 extends to agreements restraining lawful trade, including client relationships.
examples: Employee shall not solicit any clients for 3 years after leaving.
You shall not approach any customer or employee for 2 years post-termination.

rules.explanation_template: This clause imposes a geographic restriction on your ability to work{geographic_text}. Section 27 prohibits restraints on lawful profession regardless of geographic scope. Such clauses are void in India.
rules.recommendation: Remove geographic restrictions entirely. Even limited geographic non-competes are generally unenforceable under Indian law.
rules.legal_reference: Section 27 makes no exception for geographically limited restraints of trade.
examples: Employee shall not work in a similar role within 100km of Bangalore.
Prohibited from engaging in competing business anywhere in India for 1 year.

rules.explanation_template: This appears to be a non-compete clause in the context of a business sale with goodwill. Section 27 allows such restrictions ONLY when selling a business, and they must be reasonable in scope and duration.
rules.recommendation: Ensure this applies only to actual business sale scenarios and has reasonable geographic and time limits. Consult a lawyer to verify enforceability.
rules.legal_reference: Section 27 Exception: One who sells the goodwill of a business may agree with the buyer to refrain from carrying on a similar business, within specified local limits.
examples: Upon sale of business and goodwill, seller shall not compete within 50km for 2 years.
